docs.title: StaffOps
docs.text: Open source operations platform for DevOps, SRE and Platform Engineering teams.

docs.title: Why StaffOps
docs.text:
Knowledge of infrastructure operations is fragmented, expensive to access, and concentrated in large companies. Smaller teams, independent professionals, and communities don't have access to the same practices, tools, and operational intelligence that enterprises with hundreds of engineers do.

StaffOps is here to change that.

We build open source tools that bring AI-assisted operations to everyone — with or without a cloud budget, with or without a dedicated platform team. The goal is a collaborative ecosystem where the community is the real product, not the vendor.

docs.title: What we build
docs.text: Product Description Aigent Squad Multi-agent platform for AWS/Kubernetes operations. 1 supervisor + 5 specialist agents, config-driven, read-only by default. ChaiTops Conversational orchestrator for observability stacks. LibreChat frontend + FastAPI backend with multi-CLI adapter support. Anomaly Detection Distributed anomaly detection for Kubernetes. Go controller + workers + Python ML (Isolation Forest, Prophet). Agent Definition Core rules, global steering, lazy-loaded skills and agent definitions. The source of truth for all agent behavior. Helm Charts Kubernetes-ready Helm charts for the full StaffOps suite.

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
Open source. Collaborative. Free.
AI as an amplifier, not a replacement.

Everything we build is public — code, architectural decisions, learnings, and operational patterns. Anyone can contribute, adapt, and improve. No dashboards behind a paywall, no vendor lock-in.

AI is one of the means, not the end. The real product is accessible operational knowledge, built collectively and versioned like code.

Read our principles :octicons-arrow-right-24: How to contribute :octicons-arrow-right-24:

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
# Install Helm charts
helm repo add staffops https://staffops.github.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update

# Deploy Aigent Squad
helm install aigent-squad staffops/aigent-squad \
  --namespace staffops \
  --create-namespace

docs.title: Community
docs.text:
StaffOps is built in the open. Issues, discussions, and contributions happen on GitHub.

  • GitHub Organization
  • Open Issues
  • Discussions

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: StaffOps is a community project. Every contribution matters — from fixing a typo to building a new agent.

docs.title: Report an issue
docs.text:
Found a bug or something that doesn't work as documented? Open an issue in the relevant repository.

  • Aigent Squad issues
  • ChaiTops issues
  • Anomaly Detection issues
  • Helm Charts issues

Be specific: what did you expect, what happened, and how to reproduce it.

docs.title: Improve documentation
docs.text: Docs live inside each repository under docs/. If something is unclear, missing, or wrong — a pull request fixing it is always welcome.

docs.title: Submit a pull request
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository you want to contribute to
  2. Create a branch (git checkout -b feat/my-contribution)
  3. Make your changes
  4. Run existing tests if available
  5. Open a pull request with a clear description of what and why

docs.title: Share an operational learning
docs.text:
One of the most valuable things you can contribute is a real-world operational insight — a pattern that worked, a pitfall to avoid, a decision and its tradeoffs.

Open an issue or a discussion describing it and we'll document it in the project.

docs.title: Code standards
docs.text:
• Follow the conventions of the language the project uses
  • Write tests for new behavior
  • Keep changes focused — one thing per pull request
  • Prefer simple, readable code over clever abstractions

docs.title: Commit conventions
docs.text:
We use Conventional Commits:

feat(scope): add new capability
fix(scope): resolve specific bug
docs(scope): update documentation
chore(scope): maintenance task

docs.title: License
docs.text:
By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under Apache 2.0.

Copyright for all contributions belongs to StaffOps Contributors.

docs.title: Code of conduct
docs.text:
Be respectful. Be constructive. Assume good intent.

We're all here to build something useful together.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text: StaffOps is built on a few core ideas that shape every decision — technical or otherwise.

docs.title: Open by default
docs.text: Code, documentation, architectural decisions, and operational learnings are all public. There is no "enterprise edition", no feature gating, no closed roadmap. What we build, we build in the open.

docs.title: Collaboration over competition
docs.text:
Contributions are welcome from anyone — regardless of company, background, or experience level. We believe that the best operational knowledge comes from many perspectives, not from a single team.

This also means we respect and build upon other open source projects. We cite, integrate, and contribute back to the ecosystem we depend on.

docs.title: AI as a tool, not an identity
docs.text:
Automation and AI are tools in the toolbox. They amplify what a small team can operate — they don't replace judgment, ownership, or collaboration.

StaffOps works with or without AI assistance. The knowledge and patterns we document are valuable independently of how they were produced.

docs.title: Pragmatism over purity
docs.text: We ship working solutions first, optimize second, and document always. A working tool that solves a real problem is better than a perfect architecture that never ships.

docs.title: Reproducible and self-sufficient
docs.text:
Any person should be able to clone a StaffOps repository and get it running without depending on the original authors. This means:

  • Dependencies are explicit and versioned
  • Configuration is documented
  • There are no implicit tribal knowledge requirements

docs.title: Read-only by default
docs.text: Automation that touches production infrastructure is dangerous. All StaffOps agents and tools operate in read-only mode by default. Write operations require explicit opt-in, human approval, and audit trails.

docs.title: Licensed for everyone
docs.text: All StaffOps projects are licensed under Apache 2.0. This allows commercial use, modification, and distribution — with or without attribution. Copyright belongs to StaffOps Contributors, not to any single individual or company.

docs.title: Products
docs.text:
StaffOps is a suite of open source tools for infrastructure and operations teams.

Each product is independently deployable, but they're designed to work well together.

docs.title: Aigent Squad
docs.text:
Multi-agent platform for AWS and Kubernetes operations. A supervisor agent routes queries to domain specialists — AWS, Kubernetes, FinOps, DevOps, Observability — and returns consolidated, context-aware responses.

Read-only by default. Config-driven. Fully observable.

Documentation →

docs.title: ChaiTops
docs.text:
Conversational orchestrator for observability stacks. Provides a chat interface (LibreChat) backed by a FastAPI orchestrator that integrates with multiple AI CLI adapters. Designed for querying and diagnosing OpenTelemetry, VictoriaMetrics, Tempo, and Loki pipelines.

Documentation →

docs.title: Anomaly Detection
docs.text:
Distributed anomaly detection service for Kubernetes. A Go controller orchestrates parallel workers that query VictoriaMetrics (PromQL) and Loki (LogQL), then correlate signals to fire meaningful alerts through Alertmanager. A Python ML service provides adaptive detection via Isolation Forest and Prophet.

Documentation →

docs.title: Agent Definition
docs.text:
The source of truth for all StaffOps agent behavior. Defines the core agent, 11 specialist subagents, 58+ lazy-loaded skills, and 21+ global steering rules. Template-driven, version-controlled, and installable via symlinks in Claude Code or Kiro CLI.

Documentation →

docs.title: Helm Charts
docs.text:
Kubernetes-ready Helm charts for the full StaffOps suite. Published to GitHub Pages and installable with helm repo add staffops https://staffops.github.io/helm-charts/.

Charts available: aigent-squad, staffops-anomaly-detection.

Documentation →

docs.title: Agent Definition
docs.text:
The source of truth for all StaffOps agent behavior.

Full documentation

The complete documentation lives in the project repository and is available at staffops.github.io/agent-definition/.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Centralizes the definitions, rules, skills, and steering that govern how StaffOps agents behave — across any AI CLI that supports the spec (Claude Code, Kiro).

docs.title: What's included
docs.text: Component Count Description Agents 1 + 11 subagents staffops (coordinator) + domain specialists Skills 58+ Lazy-loaded knowledge, on-demand via YAML frontmatter Steering 21+ global Universal rules always active (security, git, k8s safety, 12-factor, etc.) Context Templates + overlays STAFFOPS.md generic; org-specific overlays (e.g. BDC) Learnings Structured troubleshooting, patterns, decisions, infrastructure

docs.title: Subagents
docs.text: observability · documentation · anomaly-detection · aws · finops · dev · gitops · security · sre · code-review · troubleshoot

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/StaffOps/staffops_agent_definition
cd staffops_agent_definition
./setup-agents.sh --install

Works with Claude Code and Kiro CLI via symlinks. Org-specific behavior via overlays — no changes to the core repository needed.

docs.title: Source
docs.text: github.com/StaffOps/staffops_agent_definition

docs.title: AIgent Squad
docs.text:
AIgent Squad is a multi-agent AI platform for AWS and Kubernetes operations. It receives natural-language operational questions, classifies them with a lightweight Bedrock call, and routes them to the right specialist agent — each of which collects read-only context from its data sources and synthesizes a structured answer using Claude on AWS Bedrock.

Full documentation

The complete AIgent Squad documentation lives in the project repository and is available at staffops.github.io/aigent-squad/.

docs.title: Specialist agents
docs.text: Agent Domain Supervisor Query classification, routing, and fan-out orchestration AWS EC2, EKS, IAM, S3, RDS, VPC, Cost Explorer Kubernetes Pods, deployments, services, events, metrics FinOps Cost analysis, savings plans, budget attribution DevOps CI/CD pipelines, GitLab, Jira, deployment tracking Observability Prometheus metrics, logs, traces, alert triage

docs.title: Key properties
docs.text: Property Detail Read-only by default Agents never execute mutations — analysis and recommendations only Config-driven Add an agent with two files (agent.yaml + prompt.md), no code changes Single image All agents run in-process in one container for simple deployments Cost attributed Per-agent token counting and estimated USD cost via Bedrock inference profiles Observable OTel traces, RED metrics, and structured JSON logs on every component Conversation history DynamoDB with 24 h TTL per user and session

docs.title: Quick install
docs.text:
helm repo add staffops https://staffops.github.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update

helm install aigent-squad staffops/aigent-squad \
  --namespace aigent-squad --create-namespace \
  --set redis.host=<your-elasticache-endpoint>

Docker Hub: karlipegomes/aigent-squad:latest (multi-arch amd64 + arm64)

Distributed topology available

The Helm chart supports a distributed topology where each agent runs as its own Deployment with an independent ServiceAccount and IRSA role. Pass --set topology=distributed or use the provided values-distributed.yaml.

docs.title: Source
docs.text: github.com/StaffOps/staffops-aigent-squad — Apache 2.0

docs.title: Anomaly Detection
docs.text:
Distributed anomaly detection service for Kubernetes clusters.

Full documentation

The complete documentation lives in the project repository and is available at staffops.github.io/anomaly-detection/.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Queries VictoriaMetrics and Loki continuously, detects statistical and ML-based anomalies, correlates signals across sources, and fires actionable alerts through Alertmanager.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Controller (Go, HA)
     ↓ gRPC
Workers (Go, stateless) ──→ VictoriaMetrics (PromQL)
     ↓                   ──→ Loki (LogQL)
ML Service (Python)
     ↓
Alertmanager
Component Technology Replicas Role Controller Go + gRPC 2 (HA Lease) Orchestrates detection cycles, correlates anomalies Workers Go + gRPC 3 (stateless) Executes queries, runs detection algorithms ML Service Python + FastAPI 1 Isolation Forest + Prophet forecasting Redis KV store 1 Baselines, dedup TTL, seasonal profiles

docs.title: Detection algorithms
docs.text:
• Z-Score (adaptive) — statistical threshold with seasonal adjustment
  • EWMA — exponentially weighted moving average
  • Isolation Forest — multivariate ML-based outlier detection
  • Prophet — time-series forecasting for expected vs actual comparison

docs.title: Quick install
docs.text:
helm repo add staffops https://staffops.github.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update

helm install anomaly-detection staffops/staffops-anomaly-detection \
  --namespace staffops \
  --create-namespace \
  --set victoriaMetrics.url=http://vmselect:8481/select/0/prometheus

docs.title: Source
docs.text: github.com/StaffOps/staffops-anomaly-detection

docs.title: ChaiTops
docs.text:
Conversational orchestrator for observability stacks.

Full documentation

The complete ChaiTops documentation lives in the project repository and is available at staffops.github.io/chaitops/.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: ChaiTops provides a chat interface to query and diagnose observability pipelines — OpenTelemetry, VictoriaMetrics, Tempo, Loki, and Grafana — without needing to know PromQL, LogQL, or the underlying tooling.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
LibreChat (UI) ──→ Agent API (FastAPI) ──→ Agent Adapters
                                              ├─ Claude API
                                              ├─ Kiro CLI
                                              └─ OpenCode
  • LibreChat — conversational frontend with authentication and history
  • Agent API — FastAPI orchestrator with workspace isolation per session
  • Adapters — pluggable CLI backends, CLI-agnostic by design
  • MCP Manager — Model Context Protocol server integration
  • Redis — session state, job queue, agent locks
  • DynamoDB — conversation history with 24h TTL

docs.title: Key properties
docs.text:
• Multi-CLI adapter support (Claude, Kiro, OpenCode)
  • Streaming responses for long-running tasks
  • Workspace isolation per session
  • Future-ready for Slack/Teams/Discord integration

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/StaffOps/staffops-chaitops
cd staffops-chaitops
cp .env.example .env
# fill in your credentials
docker compose up -d

docs.title: Source
docs.text: github.com/StaffOps/staffops-chaitops

docs.title: Helm Charts
docs.text:
Kubernetes-ready Helm charts for the StaffOps suite.

Full documentation

The complete documentation lives at staffops.github.io/helm-charts/ — the same URL also serves the Helm chart index (index.yaml).

docs.title: Available charts
docs.text: Chart Version Description aigent-squad 0.5.0 Multi-agent platform — supervisor + 5 specialists staffops-anomaly-detection 0.1.0 Distributed anomaly detection — Go controller + ML

docs.title: Add the repository
docs.text:
helm repo add staffops https://staffops.github.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Aigent SquadAnomaly Detection 
helm install aigent-squad staffops/aigent-squad \
  --namespace staffops \
  --create-namespace

helm install anomaly-detection staffops/staffops-anomaly-detection \
  --namespace staffops \
  --create-namespace

docs.title: CI/CD
docs.text: Charts are automatically linted, tested, and published on every merge to main via GitHub Actions using chart-testing and chart-releaser-action.
